const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: ../../[REDACTED_PHONE]/1-airm2-2-codexproject-https-github/work/CodexProject/OpenAIDatabase/tests/test_memory_atlas_r7_public_raw.py
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import base64
+import hashlib
+import importlib.util
+import json
+import re
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import tempfile
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+SCRIPTS_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / "scripts"
+CHATGPT_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_chatgpt_memory_data.py"
+CODEX_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_codex_memory_data.py"
+FUTURE_AGENT_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_future_agent_data.py"
+ATLASCTL = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "atlasctl.py"
+REGISTRY = REPO_ROOT / "机器治理" / "同步与备份" / "sync_source_registry.json"
+MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES = 40 * 1024 * 1024
+
+
+def load_script_module(filename: str, module_name: str):
+    path = SCRIPTS_ROOT / filename
+    if not path.is_file():
+        raise AssertionError(f"required script does not exist: {path}")
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_ROOT))
+    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, path)
+    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
+    assert spec and spec.loader
+    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
+    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
+    return module
+
+
+def run_json(command: list[str], *, expect_success: bool = True) -> tuple[dict, subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]]:
+    result = subprocess.run(command, cwd=REPO_ROOT, text=True, capture_output=True, check=False)
+    if expect_success and result.returncode != 0:
+        raise AssertionError(
+            f"command failed: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}\nstderr={result.stderr}"
+        )
+    if not expect_success and result.returncode == 0:
+        raise AssertionError(f"command unexpectedly passed: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}")
+    start = result.stdout.find("{")
+    if start < 0:
+        raise AssertionError(
+            f"command did not emit JSON: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}\nstderr={result.stderr}"
+        )
+    return json.loads(result.stdout[start:]), result
+
+
+def write_jsonl(path: Path, rows: list[dict]) -> Non[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    path.write_text(
+        "".join(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True) + "\n" for row in rows),
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+
+
+def write_chatgpt_export(path: Path) -> Non[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]    payload = [
+        {
+            "id": "conv_r7",
+            "title": "Owner [REDACTED_EMAIL] at [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
+            "create_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
+            "update_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
+            "mapping": {
+                "root": {
+                    "id": "root",
+                    "message": {
+                        "id": "msg_user",
+                        "author": {"role": "user"},
+                        "create_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
+                        "content": {
+                            "content_type": "text",
+                            "parts": [
+                                "Call [REDACTED_PHONE]; [REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]; keep the report text."
+                            ],
+                        },
+                    },
+                }
+            },
+        }
+    ]
+    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def write_codex_session(codex_home: Path) -> tuple[Path, list[dict]]:
+    session_id = "01999999-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-[REDACTED_PHONE]"
+    rows = [
+        {
+            "type": "session_meta",
+            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:00:00Z",
+            "payload": {
+                "id": session_id,
+                "cwd": "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
+                "originator": "codex_cli",
+            },
+        },
+        {
+            "type": "response_item",
+            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:01:00Z",
+            "payload": {
+                "type": "message",
+                "role": "user",
+                "content": [
+                    {
+                        "type": "input_text",
+                        "text": "Use [REDACTED_CREDENTIAL] and read [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
+                    }
+                ],
+            },
+        },
+        {
+            "type": "response_item",
+            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:02:00Z",
+            "payload": {
+                "type": "function_call_output",
+                "call_id": "call_r7",
+                "output": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAAB",
+            },
+        },
+        {
+            "type": "event_msg",
+            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:03:00Z",
+            "payload": {"type": "agent_message", "message": "ordinary complete tool result"},
+        },
+    ]
+    write_jsonl(codex_home / "session_index.jsonl", [{"id": session_id, "thread_name": "R7 public raw"}])
+    source = codex_home / "sessions" / "2026" / "07" / "11" / "session.jsonl"
+    write_jsonl(source, rows)
+    return source, rows
+
+
+class PublicRawSanitizerTest(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_recursive_redaction_replaces_credentials_pii_and_absolute_paths(self) -> Non[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        sanitizer = load_script_module("public_raw_sanitizer.py", "public_raw_sanitizer_r7_redaction")
+        value = {
+            "message": "[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL] [REDACTED_EMAIL] [REDACTED_PHONE]",
+            "nested": ["[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", {"email": "[REDACTED_EMAIL]"}],
+        }
+
+        sanitized, counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value(value)
+        serialized = json.dumps(sanitized, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
+
+        self.assertNotIn("correct-horse-battery", serialized)
+        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
+        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
+        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", serialized)
+        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", serialized)
+        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", serialized)
+        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
+        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", serialized)
+        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", serialized)
+        self.assertEqual(counts["credential_passwords"], 1)
+        self.assertEqual(counts["email"], 2)
+        self.assertEqual(counts["phone"], 1)
+        self.assertEqual(counts["local_absolute_path"], 1)
+
+    def test_binary_markers_are_deterministic_and_ordinary_long_text_is_unchanged(self) -> Non[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        sanitizer = load_script_module("public_raw_sanitizer.py", "public_raw_sanitizer_r7_binary")
+        data_url = "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAAB"
+        marker = sanitizer.binary_omission_marker(data_url)
+        expected_sha = hashlib.sha256(data_url.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
+        self.assertEqual(marker, sanitizer.binary_omission_marker(data_url))
+        self.assertIn(f"sha256={expected_sha}", marker)
+        self.assertIn(f"bytes={len(data_url.encode('utf-8'))}", marker)
+        self.assertIn("reason=non_text_binary_not_transcript", marker)
+
+        base64_blob = base64.b64encode(b"\x00\xff" * (sanitizer.BINARY_STRING_MIN_BYTES // 2)).decode("ascii")
+        sanitized, counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value([data_url, base64_blob])
+        self.assertTrue(all(item.startswith("[OMITTED_BINARY ") for item in sanitized))
+        self.assertEqual(counts["binary_omission"], 2)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            counts["binary_omitted_bytes"],
+            len(data_url.encode("utf-8")) + len(base64_blob.encode("utf-8")),
+        )
+
+        ordinary_long_text = "This is an ordinary report paragraph with spaces. " * 8000
+        ordinary, ordinary_counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value(ordinary_long_text)
+        self.assertEqual(ordinary, ordinary_long_text)
+        self.assertEqual(ordinary_counts, {})
+
+
+class PublicRawConnectorTest(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_chatgpt_public_backup_requires_explicit_redaction_and_writes_versioned_manifest(self) -> Non[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdi[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]            root = Path(tmpdir)
+            export_path = root / "official-export.json"
+            write_chatgpt_export(export_path)
+
+            strict, _ = run_json(
+                [
+                    sys.executable,
+                    str(CHATGPT_SYNC),
+                    "--database-dir",
+                    str(root),
+                    "--official-export",
+                    str(export_path),
+                ],
+                expect_success=False,
+            )
+            self.assertEqual(strict["reason"], "credential_is_not_memory")
+            self.assertFalse((root / "data/public_raw/chatgpt").exists())
+
+            result, _ = run_json(
+                [
+                    sys.executable,
+                    str(CHATGPT_SYNC),
+                    "--database-dir",
+                    str(root),
+                    "--official-export",
+                    str(export_path),
+                    "--redact-for-public-backup",
+                ]
+            )
+
+            raw_files = list((root / "data/public_raw/chatgpt").glob("conv_r7.*.json"))
+            self.assertEqual(len(raw_files), 1)
+            self.assertRegex(raw_files[0].name, r"^conv_r7\.[0-9a-f]{12}\.json$")
+            raw_text = raw_files[0].read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", raw_text)
+            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", raw_text)
+            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", raw_text)
+            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", raw_text)
+
+            manifest_path = root / "data/processed/conversations/conversation_manifest.jsonl"
+            manifest_rows = [json.loads(line) for line in manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()]
+            self.assertEqual(len(manifest_rows), 1)
+            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["conversation_id"], "conv_r7")
+            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["raw_ref"], result["raw_paths"][0])
+            export_sha = hashlib.sha256(export_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
+            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["export_sha256"], export_sha)
+            self.assertEqual(result["export_sha256"], export_sha)
+            self.assertEqual(result["processed_manifest"], "data/processed/conversations/conversation_manifest.jsonl")
+            self.assertTrue(result["redact_for_public_backup"])
+            self.assertGreater(sum(result["redaction_counts"].values()), 0)
+
+            source_absolute = str(export_path.resolve())
+            public_outputs = "\n".join(
+                [
+                    json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True),
+                    raw_text,
+                    manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
+                    (root / "data/derived/chatgpt/chatgpt_sync_summary.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
+                ]
+            )
+            self.assertNotIn(source_absolute, public_outputs)
+            self.assertLessEqual(raw_files[0].stat().st_size, MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES)

from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import hashlib
import importlib.util
import json
import re
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
import unittest
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
SCRIPTS_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / "scripts"
CHATGPT_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_chatgpt_memory_data.py"
CODEX_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_codex_memory_data.py"
FUTURE_AGENT_SYNC = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "sync_future_agent_data.py"
ATLASCTL = SCRIPTS_ROOT / "atlasctl.py"
REGISTRY = REPO_ROOT / "机器治理" / "同步与备份" / "sync_source_registry.json"
MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES = 40 * 1024 * 1024


def load_script_module(filename: str, module_name: str):
    path = SCRIPTS_ROOT / filename
    if not path.is_file():
        raise AssertionError(f"required script does not exist: {path}")
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_ROOT))
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, path)
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    assert spec and spec.loader
    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    return module


def run_json(command: list[str], *, expect_success: bool = True) -> tuple[dict, subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]]:
    result = subprocess.run(command, cwd=REPO_ROOT, text=True, capture_output=True, check=False)
    if expect_success and result.returncode != 0:
        raise AssertionError(
            f"command failed: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}\nstderr={result.stderr}"
        )
    if not expect_success and result.returncode == 0:
        raise AssertionError(f"command unexpectedly passed: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}")
    start = result.stdout.find("{")
    if start < 0:
        raise AssertionError(
            f"command did not emit JSON: {command}\nstdout={result.stdout}\nstderr={result.stderr}"
        )
    return json.loads(result.stdout[start:]), result


def write_jsonl(path: Path, rows: list[dict]) -> None:
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(
        "".join(json.dumps(row, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True) + "\n" for row in rows),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )


def write_chatgpt_export(path: Path) -> None:
    payload = [
        {
            "id": "conv_r7",
            "title": "Owner [REDACTED_EMAIL] at [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
            "create_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
            "update_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
            "mapping": {
                "root": {
                    "id": "root",
                    "message": {
                        "id": "msg_user",
                        "author": {"role": "user"},
                        "create_time": [REDACTED_PHONE],
                        "content": {
                            "content_type": "text",
                            "parts": [
                                "Call [REDACTED_PHONE]; [REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]; keep the report text."
                            ],
                        },
                    },
                }
            },
        }
    ]
    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")


def write_codex_session(codex_home: Path) -> tuple[Path, list[dict]]:
    session_id = "01999999-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-[REDACTED_PHONE]"
    rows = [
        {
            "type": "session_meta",
            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:00:00Z",
            "payload": {
                "id": session_id,
                "cwd": "[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
                "originator": "codex_cli",
            },
        },
        {
            "type": "response_item",
            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:01:00Z",
            "payload": {
                "type": "message",
                "role": "user",
                "content": [
                    {
                        "type": "input_text",
                        "text": "Use [REDACTED_CREDENTIAL] and read [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]",
                    }
                ],
            },
        },
        {
            "type": "response_item",
            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:02:00Z",
            "payload": {
                "type": "function_call_output",
                "call_id": "call_r7",
                "output": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAAB",
            },
        },
        {
            "type": "event_msg",
            "timestamp": "[REDACTED_PHONE]T00:03:00Z",
            "payload": {"type": "agent_message", "message": "ordinary complete tool result"},
        },
    ]
    write_jsonl(codex_home / "session_index.jsonl", [{"id": session_id, "thread_name": "R7 public raw"}])
    source = codex_home / "sessions" / "2026" / "07" / "11" / "session.jsonl"
    write_jsonl(source, rows)
    return source, rows


class PublicRawSanitizerTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_recursive_redaction_replaces_credentials_pii_and_absolute_paths(self) -> None:
        sanitizer = load_script_module("public_raw_sanitizer.py", "public_raw_sanitizer_r7_redaction")
        value = {
            "message": "[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL] [REDACTED_EMAIL] [REDACTED_PHONE]",
            "nested": ["[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", {"email": "[REDACTED_EMAIL]"}],
        }

        sanitized, counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value(value)
        serialized = json.dumps(sanitized, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)

        self.assertNotIn("correct-horse-battery", serialized)
        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", serialized)
        self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", serialized)
        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", serialized)
        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", serialized)
        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", serialized)
        self.assertIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", serialized)
        self.assertEqual(counts["credential_passwords"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(counts["email"], 2)
        self.assertEqual(counts["phone"], 1)
        self.assertEqual(counts["local_absolute_path"], 1)

    def test_binary_markers_are_deterministic_and_ordinary_long_text_is_unchanged(self) -> None:
        sanitizer = load_script_module("public_raw_sanitizer.py", "public_raw_sanitizer_r7_binary")
        data_url = "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAAB"
        marker = sanitizer.binary_omission_marker(data_url)
        expected_sha = hashlib.sha256(data_url.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
        self.assertEqual(marker, sanitizer.binary_omission_marker(data_url))
        self.assertIn(f"sha256={expected_sha}", marker)
        self.assertIn(f"bytes={len(data_url.encode('utf-8'))}", marker)
        self.assertIn("reason=non_text_binary_not_transcript", marker)

        base64_blob = base64.b64encode(b"\x00\xff" * (sanitizer.BINARY_STRING_MIN_BYTES // 2)).decode("ascii")
        sanitized, counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value([data_url, base64_blob])
        self.assertTrue(all(item.startswith("[OMITTED_BINARY ") for item in sanitized))
        self.assertEqual(counts["binary_omission"], 2)
        self.assertEqual(
            counts["binary_omitted_bytes"],
            len(data_url.encode("utf-8")) + len(base64_blob.encode("utf-8")),
        )

        ordinary_long_text = "This is an ordinary report paragraph with spaces. " * 8000
        ordinary, ordinary_counts = sanitizer.sanitize_public_value(ordinary_long_text)
        self.assertEqual(ordinary, ordinary_long_text)
        self.assertEqual(ordinary_counts, {})


class PublicRawConnectorTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_chatgpt_public_backup_requires_explicit_redaction_and_writes_versioned_manifest(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
            root = Path(tmpdir)
            export_path = root / "official-export.json"
            write_chatgpt_export(export_path)

            strict, _ = run_json(
                [
                    sys.executable,
                    str(CHATGPT_SYNC),
                    "--database-dir",
                    str(root),
                    "--official-export",
                    str(export_path),
                ],
                expect_success=False,
            )
            self.assertEqual(strict["reason"], "credential_is_not_memory")
            self.assertFalse((root / "data/public_raw/chatgpt").exists())

            result, _ = run_json(
                [
                    sys.executable,
                    str(CHATGPT_SYNC),
                    "--database-dir",
                    str(root),
                    "--official-export",
                    str(export_path),
                    "--redact-for-public-backup",
                ]
            )

            raw_files = list((root / "data/public_raw/chatgpt").glob("conv_r7.*.json"))
            self.assertEqual(len(raw_files), 1)
            self.assertRegex(raw_files[0].name, r"^conv_r7\.[0-9a-f]{12}\.json$")
            raw_text = raw_files[0].read_text(encoding="utf-8")
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", raw_text)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", raw_text)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_PHONE]", raw_text)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", raw_text)

            manifest_path = root / "data/processed/conversations/conversation_manifest.jsonl"
            manifest_rows = [json.loads(line) for line in manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()]
            self.assertEqual(len(manifest_rows), 1)
            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["conversation_id"], "conv_r7")
            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["raw_ref"], result["raw_paths"][0])
            export_sha = hashlib.sha256(export_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
            self.assertEqual(manifest_rows[0]["export_sha256"], export_sha)
            self.assertEqual(result["export_sha256"], export_sha)
            self.assertEqual(result["processed_manifest"], "data/processed/conversations/conversation_manifest.jsonl")
            self.assertTrue(result["redact_for_public_backup"])
            self.assertGreater(sum(result["redaction_counts"].values()), 0)

            source_absolute = str(export_path.resolve())
            public_outputs = "\n".join(
                [
                    json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True),
                    raw_text,
                    manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
                    (root / "data/derived/chatgpt/chatgpt_sync_summary.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
                ]
            )
            self.assertNotIn(source_absolute, public_outputs)
            self.assertLessEqual(raw_files[0].stat().st_size, MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES)

    def test_codex_public_transcripts_preserve_every_event_with_relative_provenance(self) -> None:
        module = load_script_module("sync_codex_memory_data.py", "sync_codex_memory_data_r7")
        self.assertTrue(callable(module.export_codex_session_jsonl))
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
            root = Path(tmpdir)
            database = root / "db"
            codex_home = root / ".codex"
            _source, source_rows = write_codex_session(codex_home)

            result = module.sync_codex_data(
                database,
                codex_home,
                build_atlas=False,
                commit=False,
                push=False,
                force_full_scan=True,
                public_transcripts=True,
            )

            snapshot_path = database / result["outputs"]["public_raw_snapshot"]
            snapshot = json.loads(snapshot_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            self.assertTrue(snapshot["public_transcripts_included"])
            self.assertEqual(snapshot["public_transcript_event_count"], len(source_rows))
            self.assertEqual(len(snapshot["public_transcript_exports"]), 1)
            export = snapshot["public_transcript_exports"][0]
            self.assertEqual(export["source_relative_path"], "sessions/2026/07/11/session.jsonl")
            self.assertEqual(export["event_count"], len(source_rows))
            self.assertEqual(export["chunk_count"], len(export["chunk_refs"]))

            chunk_paths = [database / ref for ref in export["chunk_refs"]]
            self.assertTrue(all(path.is_file() for path in chunk_paths))
            self.assertTrue(
                all(
                    re.match(
                        r"^01999999-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-[REDACTED_PHONE]\.[0-9a-f]{12}\.part-\d{4}\.jsonl$",
                        path.name,
                    )
                    for path in chunk_paths
                )
            )
            exported_rows = []
            for path in chunk_paths:
                self.assertLessEqual(path.stat().st_size, MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES)
                exported_rows.extend(json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines())
            self.assertEqual(len(exported_rows), len(source_rows))
            exported_text = json.dumps(exported_rows, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", exported_text)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", exported_text)
            self.assertIn("[OMITTED_BINARY ", exported_text)
            self.assertNotIn("correct-horse-battery", exported_text)
            self.assertNotIn("[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]", exported_text)
            self.assertNotIn(str(codex_home.resolve()), json.dumps(snapshot, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True))
            self.assertTrue(result["public_transcripts"])

    def test_future_agent_markdown_import_is_versioned_and_registry_keeps_template(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
            root = Path(tmpdir)

            report.write_text(
                "# R6 Independent Review\n\nReviewed [REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH] "

            self.assertEqual(payload["event_id"], "r6-independent-review")
            self.assertEqual(payload["source_format"], "markdown_report")
            payload_text = json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_CREDENTIAL]", payload_text)
            self.assertIn("[REDACTED_EMAIL]", payload_text)

        registry = json.loads(REGISTRY.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

        self.assertEqual(sources["codex-reviewer"]["source_type"], "other_agent")
        self.assertEqual(sources["codex-reviewer"]["raw_root"], "data/public_raw/agents/codex-reviewer")

    def test_atlasctl_wires_all_r7_connector_flags_in_dry_run_mode(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:

+#!/usr/bin/env python3

+from __future__ import annotations

+BINARY_STRING_MIN_BYTES = 256 * 1024

+BASE64_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9+/_-]+={0,2}$")
+
+
+class PublicRawLimitError(ValueError):
+    pass
+
+
+class PublicRawSanitizationError(ValueError):
+    pass
+
+
+def merge_counts(left: dict[str, int], right: dict[str, int]) -> dict[str, int]:
+    merged = dict(left)
+    for key, value in right.items():
+        merged[key] = merged.get(key, 0) + int(value)
+    return merged
+
+

+        "reason=non_text_binary_not_transcript]"
+    )

+
+def is_non_text_binary_string(value: str) -> boo[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]    if DATA_URL_RE.match(value):
+        return True
+    raw_size = len(value.encode("utf-8"))

+        return False
+    candidate = value.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "")
+    if len(candidate) < BINARY_STRING_MIN_BYTES or len(candidate) % 4 != 0:
+        return False
+    if not BASE64_RE.fullmatch(candidate):
+        return False
+    tr[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        decoded = base64.b64decode(candidate, altchars=b"-_", validate=True)
+    except (binascii.Error, ValueError):
+        return False
+    return bool(decoded)
+
+
+def sanitize_public_value(value: Any) -> tuple[Any, dict[str, int]]:
+    if isinstance(value, str):
+        if is_non_text_binary_string(value):
+            return binary_omission_marker(value), {
+                "binary_omission": 1,
+                "binary_omitted_bytes": len(value.encode("utf-8")),
+            }
+        return redact_text(value)
+
+    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
+        sanitized_items: list[Any] = []
+        counts: dict[str, int] = {}
+        for item in valu[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]            sanitized_item, item_counts = sanitize_public_value(item)
+            sanitized_items.append(sanitized_item)
+            counts = merge_counts(counts, item_counts)
+        return sanitized_items, counts
+
+    if isinstance(value, dict):
+        sanitized_dict: dict[str, Any] = {}
+        counts: dict[str, int] = {}
+        for key, item in value.items():
+            sanitized_key, key_counts = sanitize_public_value(str(key))
+            if sanitized_key in sanitized_dic[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]                raise PublicRawSanitizationError(
+                    f"sanitized object key collision: {sanitized_key}"
+                )
+            sanitized_item, item_counts = sanitize_public_value(item)
+            sanitized_dict[sanitized_key] = sanitized_item
+            counts = merge_counts(counts, key_counts)
+            counts = merge_counts(counts, item_counts)
+        return sanitized_dict, counts
+
+    return value, {}
+
+
+def sanitize_jsonl_event(event: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, int]]:
+    sanitized, counts = sanitize_public_value(event)
+    if not isinstance(sanitized, dict):
+        raise PublicRawSanitizationError("sanitized JSONL event must remain an object")
+    return sanitized, counts
+
+
+def require_public_raw_file_size(payload: bytes | str, label: str) -> in[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]    encoded = payload.encode("utf-8") if isinstance(payload, str) else payload
+    size = len(encoded)
+    if size > MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTE[REDACTED_LOCAL_PATH]        raise PublicRawLimitError(
+            f"{label} is {size} bytes; public raw limit is {MAX_PUBLIC_RAW_FILE_BYTES} bytes"

+    return size

+def markdown_title(text: str) -> str:
+    for line in text.splitlines():
+        candidate = line.strip()
+        if candidate.startswith("#"):
+            title = candidate.lstrip("#").strip()
+            if title:
+                return title
+        if candidate:
+            return candidate[:160]
+    return "Untitled future agent Markdown report"
+
+
+def normalize_markdown_event(agent_id: str, event_id: str, text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    if not text.strip():
+        raise PublicRawSanitizationError("Markdown report must not be empty")

+        "schema_version": "memory_atlas_public_raw_future_agent.v1",
+        "source_id": SOURCE_ID,
+        "agent_id": agent_id,
+        "adapter_mode": ADAPTER_MODE,
+        "event_id": event_id,
+        "title": markdown_title(text),
+        "message_count": 1,
+        "messages": [
+            {
+                "message_id": f"{event_id}-report",
+                "role": "assistant",
+                "text": text,
+            }
+        ],
+        "credential_boundary": "credentials_not_transcript",
+        "sync_mode": "manual_markdown_import",
+        "source_format": "markdown_report",

Output:

# R6 Task 1 Report: Redacted Facet And Formula Snapshot Contract

## Scope

Implemented only R6 Task 1 in `OpenAIDatabase`. The change adds the public,
read-only snapshot contracts for visual workflow metadata, behavior-event facets,
and Formula What-If preview data. No raw/private source data, UI workflow files,
package files, deployment state, or R7/Task 2 work was changed.

## Delivered Contract

- `visual_workflows` reads the tracked Human Question Map through a strict
  whitelist. It fails closed unless the source has the required schema, exact
  filter dimensions, and the exact twelve P0 IDs.
- `behavior_intelligence.facet_events` exports only the ten R6 facet fields,
  stable-sorts valid timestamps before invalid timestamps, limits output to
  1,000 events, limits evidence references to three, normalizes blank/unknown
  project and task values to `未标注`, and provides deterministic source/project/
  task options.
- `formula_what_if` exports only the preview's defaults, bounded controls,
  baseline signals, rework score, relative formula source, scenarios, and
  explicit no-write/no-financial-advice safety flags.
- `apps/memory-atlas/src/types.ts` exports `VisualFacetEvent`,
  `VisualWorkflowRegistry`, and `FormulaWhatIfPreview`, with the requested
  optional `MemoryAtlas` and `BehaviorIntelligenceSummary` fields.

## Rebuilt Snapshot

`data/derived/visualization/memory_atlas.json` was rebuilt from the canonical
checkout. Its R6 contract contains:

- 12 P0 visual workflow IDs in the approved order.
- 217 bounded facet events, with source/project/task option sets derived from
  those events.
- 5 sanitized Formula What-If scenarios and explicit safety values:
  `active_config_write=false`, `proposal_required_before_apply=true`,
  `raw_mutation=false`, `financial_advice=false`, and
  `precise_income_prediction=false`.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
python3 -m unittest tests.test_memory_atlas_visual_workflows -q
Ran 1 test: OK

python3 -m unittest tests.test_memory_atlas_data -q
Ran 3 tests: OK

python3 -m json.tool data/derived/visualization/memory_atlas.json >/dev/null
exit 0

git diff --check
exit 0
```

The tracked source inputs were read-only throughout and retained their pre-build
SHA-256 values:

```text
human_question_map.v1_2_s11_p4.json  8b376a9daefee81c7d5bdcceec3dbefd71f77086a12f76e35eb1df4bae7d0c89
events.json                           03e9f4b1cd4a14c82064099ac0321107a0974aaa85503e441a62b17b70a53078
formula_what_if_preview.json          7e60f7bd0441ffcbf35381c419a620cc21dea3cc9efa30bfdd9ed4f009a47002
```

## Privacy Inspection

The rebuilt R6-only snapshot sections were inspected after serialization. Their
object keys are limited to the allowed visual, facet-event, evidence-reference,
and formula-summary fields. No transcript/message/credential/token/cookie/raw
payload keys, absolute paths, test private sentinels, or active-config target
fields were exported. A general topical use of the word `token` may occur in
allowed derived topic text; it is not a credential field or value.

## Scope Boundary

Existing unowned worktree changes in `apps/memory-atlas/package.json`,
`apps/memory-atlas/scripts/validate_memory_atlas_v1_2_visual_models.mjs`, and
`apps/memory-atlas/src/visualWorkflows.ts` were preserved and excluded from this
task's commit.
